description_long: This is a long, single-story bungalow with a gable roof running the length of the structure. The exterior walls are finished in a light tan or beige material, likely wood or stucco, with a prominent overhanging roof in a muted gray. The building sits on a rectangular plot with a paved area, possibly for parking, along one side. Windows are distributed along the length of the building, with a few grouped together and others spaced apart, providing natural light to the interior. The ends of the bungalow feature triangular gable faces, and there are no visible porches or verandas. The overall design is simple and functional, typical of suburban or residential developments.
description: A long, single-story bungalow with a gray gable roof and tan exterior walls, set on a rectangular plot with adjacent paved parking. Windows are spaced along the length, and the design is simple and functional.
description_view_0: This view shows the side and one gable end of the bungalow, highlighting the overhanging roof and the paved area beside the building.
description_view_1: This angle emphasizes the opposite gable end and the length of the building, with the roof's slope and window arrangement visible.
description_view_2: This perspective displays the full length of the bungalow and the adjacent parking area, with the roof and window distribution clearly seen.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the long side of the bungalow, showing the row of windows and the full extent of the paved area.
